Never assume synchronized clocks for correctness; use logical clocks, version vectors, or hybrid logical clocks for ordering and conflict resolution.
Bound and handle failure with timeouts, retries with jittered backoff, circuit breakers, and backpressure so a slow dependency cannot cascade into an outage.
Make replication and partitioning explicit: pick a partition/sharding key, plan rebalancing, and account for replication lag in read paths.
Test under failure with deterministic simulation, fault injection, and chaos testing, and verify safety/liveness properties rather than only happy-path behavior.
Keep operations observable end-to-end with distributed tracing and per-node metrics so split-brain, lag, and hotspots are detectable.
scaffolds: Scaffold a replicated service with a consensus/coordination layer for leader election and a replicated log.
Generate an idempotent RPC layer with retries, jittered backoff, deadlines, and circuit breaking.
Build a sharding/partitioning module with a routing key, rebalancing plan, and replica placement.
Create a fault-injection and deterministic-simulation test harness verifying safety and liveness.

name: Search & Information Retrieval
description: Full-text and semantic search, relevance ranking, indexing, and retrieval systems.
conventions: Design the analysis pipeline deliberately (tokenization, lowercasing, stemming, stop-words, synonyms) and apply the same analyzer at index and query time.
Model the index schema explicitly: choose field types, what is indexed versus stored, and analyzers per field rather than defaulting everything.
Evaluate relevance with labeled judgments and offline metrics (precision/recall, NDCG, MRR) before and after ranking changes, not by spot-checking.
Combine lexical and semantic retrieval (hybrid search) and re-rank candidates, since neither keyword nor vector search alone is best for all queries.
Tune ranking with field boosts, recency, and learned/business signals, and keep ranking logic versioned and testable.
Make indexing incremental and idempotent, handle updates/deletes, and plan reindexing with zero-downtime alias swaps.
Bound query cost: paginate efficiently (search-after over deep offset), cap aggregations, and add timeouts so expensive queries cannot overload the cluster.
Handle typos, partial matches, and faceting/filtering as first-class features, and monitor query latency and zero-result rates.
scaffolds: Scaffold a search index with an explicit schema, analyzer configuration, and an idempotent incremental indexer.
Generate a hybrid (lexical plus vector) retrieval pipeline with a re-ranking stage.
Build a relevance-evaluation harness with labeled judgments and offline ranking metrics.
Create a faceted-search API with filtering, pagination via search-after, and query timeouts.

name: Payments & Billing
description: Payment processing, subscriptions, invoicing, and financial integrations with correctness and idempotency.
conventions: Represent money as integer minor units (or a decimal type) with an explicit currency; never use floating point for monetary amounts.
Make every payment operation idempotent with idempotency keys so client/network retries cannot double-charge.
Treat the payment provider's webhooks as the source of truth for state transitions, verify webhook signatures, and process them idempotently and out of order.
Never store raw card data; tokenize through the provider and keep the integration out of PCI scope by using hosted fields/checkout.
Reconcile your ledger against the provider regularly and record an immutable, append-only audit trail of every financial event.
Handle the full lifecycle explicitly: authorization, capture, partial/refund, chargeback, retries, and failed renewals with dunning.
Compute tax, currency conversion, and proration deliberately and from authoritative sources rather than ad hoc math.
Secure keys and webhooks, rate-limit, and fail safe: on uncertainty, do not grant entitlements until payment is confirmed.
scaffolds: Scaffold a checkout flow using hosted/tokenized payment fields with idempotency keys and confirmation handling.
Generate a signature-verified, idempotent webhook handler that drives entitlement state transitions.
Build a subscription/billing module with proration, dunning, and an append-only ledger.
Create a reconciliation job that compares the internal ledger against provider records and flags discrepancies.

name: Compilers & Language Tooling
description: Parsers, interpreters, compilers, language servers, and static-analysis tooling.
conventions: Separate the pipeline into clear phases (lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, optimization, code generation) with well-defined intermediate representations between them.
Produce precise, recoverable error diagnostics with source spans, and continue parsing after errors so tools can report many problems at once.
Preserve source location information through every phase so errors, formatting, and tooling map back to the original text accurately.
Make the AST/IR immutable or use explicit transformation passes, and keep each pass focused and independently testable.
Design for incrementality and resilience in language servers: reparse/recheck only what changed and tolerate partial/invalid input.
Define the grammar and semantics precisely and test against a comprehensive corpus including edge cases and known-tricky constructs.
Use generated lexers/parsers or proven parsing techniques deliberately, and document the language's formal grammar.
Snapshot-test transformations and golden-test generated output so changes to passes are reviewable and regressions are caught.
scaffolds: Scaffold a compiler/interpreter pipeline with a lexer, parser, AST, and a tree-walking or bytecode evaluator.
Generate an error-diagnostic system with source spans, recovery, and multi-error reporting.
Build a language-server skeleton with incremental parsing, diagnostics, hover, and completion.
Create a transformation-pass framework with snapshot tests over the intermediate representation.

name: API Clients & SDKs
description: Client libraries and SDKs that wrap remote APIs with ergonomic, resilient, well-typed interfaces.
conventions: Expose a small, discoverable, strongly-typed surface that mirrors the API's resources, and generate types/clients from the API contract where possible.
Build in resilience: configurable timeouts, retries with exponential backoff and jitter on transient/5xx errors, and respect of rate-limit and retry-after headers.
Surface rich, typed errors that distinguish client (4xx), server (5xx), network, and validation failures, and include status, request id, and body.
Make the client safe for retries with idempotency keys on mutating calls and never retry non-idempotent operations blindly.
Support pagination ergonomically (auto-paginating iterators) and streaming for large responses instead of buffering everything in memory.
Handle authentication transparently with pluggable credential providers and automatic token refresh, and never log secrets or full tokens.
Keep the client side-effect-free and configurable (base URL, HTTP client injection) for testing, and ship recorded/mocked fixtures.
Version the SDK with semantic versioning, document every public method, and provide migration notes for breaking changes.
scaffolds: Scaffold a typed API client generated from an API contract with resource modules and auth handling.
Generate a resilient HTTP layer with timeouts, retry/backoff, rate-limit handling, and typed errors.
Build an auto-paginating iterator and a streaming response helper for large payloads.
Create a recorded-fixture test suite that exercises the client against captured HTTP interactions.

name: Data Visualization & Charting
description: Interactive charts, dashboards, and data-driven graphics for the web and analytics tools.
conventions: Match the chart type to the data and the question (comparison, trend, distribution, part-to-whole, correlation) rather than defaulting to one form.
Keep encodings honest: start bar axes at zero, use perceptually-uniform color scales, avoid dual axes and 3D effects that distort comparison, and label units.
Maximize the data-to-ink ratio: remove chartjunk and redundant gridlines, and let the data, not decoration, carry the message.
Make charts accessible: sufficient color contrast, non-color-only encodings, readable labels, and text/table alternatives for screen readers.
Use scales and axes deliberately (linear/log/time/ordinal), format ticks and dates legibly, and handle missing or zero data explicitly.
Separate data transformation from rendering so the same pipeline can drive different views, and keep visual encodings declarative where possible.
Optimize for large datasets with aggregation, sampling, or canvas/WebGL rendering instead of thousands of DOM nodes, and virtualize where needed.
Design responsive, interactive charts (tooltips, zoom, filtering) that degrade gracefully and remain legible at all sizes.
scaffolds: Scaffold a reusable chart component with declarative encodings, responsive sizing, and accessible labels/alt text.
Generate a dashboard layout composing multiple linked charts with shared filters and a data layer.
Build a large-dataset visualization using canvas/WebGL rendering with aggregation and level-of-detail.
Create an interactive exploration view with tooltips, zoom/pan, and cross-filtering.

name: Content Management & Headless CMS
description: Structured content modeling, headless/decoupled CMS, and content delivery for editorial workflows.
conventions: Model content as structured, typed fields with reusable components and references, not as blobs of markup, so the same content can render across channels.
Decouple content from presentation: deliver content via API and keep rendering in the front end so one source serves web, mobile, and other channels.
Version and preview content with draft/published states and scheduled publishing, and give editors a safe preview before going live.
Treat the content schema as code: version it, migrate it deliberately, and validate content against it rather than allowing arbitrary shapes.
Optimize delivery with CDN caching and incremental/on-demand revalidation, invalidating only the content that changed on publish.
Manage media as first-class assets with transformations, responsive variants, and a digital-asset pipeline rather than raw uploads.
Enforce role-based editorial permissions and an audit trail, and localize content with a clear translation workflow.
Sanitize and constrain rich-text/portable-text output to prevent injection, and keep embeds and components allow-listed.
scaffolds: Scaffold a headless-CMS content model with typed fields, reusable components, references, and localization.
Generate a content-delivery layer with CDN caching and on-publish revalidation/invalidation.
Build a draft/preview workflow with scheduled publishing and editorial roles.
Create a content-migration script that evolves the schema and backfills existing entries safely.
